name: Golden Retriever
overview: Golden Retrievers are one of the most popular family dogs in the US, known for their patience with children and eagerness to please. They were originally bred in Scotland in the 1800s for retrieving waterfowl.

name: Labrador Retriever
overview: Labradors have been the most registered breed with the AKC for over three decades. Originally from Newfoundland, they were bred to help fishermen haul nets.

name: French Bulldog
overview: French Bulldogs are a brachycephalic (flat-faced) breed, which makes them prone to breathing difficulty, especially in heat -- a key factor for owners to plan around.

name: German Shepherd
overview: Originally bred in Germany for herding sheep, German Shepherds are now widely used in police, military, and service-dog roles due to their trainability.

name: Poodle
overview: Poodles come in three official sizes and are considered one of the most trainable breeds. Their curly, low-shedding coat is a common choice for allergy-sensitive households.

name: Beagle
overview: Beagles were bred as scent hounds for tracking small game and have one of the strongest noses in the dog world, which drives much of their behavior.

name: Dachshund
overview: Bred in Germany to hunt badgers, the Dachshund's long spine and short legs make it especially prone to back injuries, so owners are advised to limit jumping.

name: Bulldog
overview: The English Bulldog's distinctive flat face and stocky build come with significant breathing and heat-tolerance considerations that owners should research before adopting.

name: Border Collie
overview: Widely regarded as the most intelligent dog breed, Border Collies were bred for herding sheep and need substantial daily mental and physical exercise.

name: Chihuahua
overview: Named after the Mexican state of Chihuahua, this is the smallest recognized dog breed and one of the longest-lived, though small size makes dental care especially important.

name: Siberian Husky
overview: Bred by the Chukchi people of Siberia to pull sleds over long distances, huskies have high energy needs and a thick double coat suited to cold climates.

name: Boxer
overview: Boxers are a brachycephalic working breed known for their playful, high-energy temperament and strong loyalty to family.

name: Australian Shepherd
overview: Despite the name, Australian Shepherds were developed in the western United States to herd livestock and remain one of the most active herding breeds.

name: Cavalier King Charles Spaniel
overview: Named for King Charles II of England, this breed is prone to a serious heart condition (mitral valve disease) that owners should discuss with a vet early.

name: Doberman Pinscher
overview: Developed in Germany in the 1890s by a tax collector for personal protection, Dobermans are now common family and working dogs known for loyalty and alertness.

name: Great Dane
overview: One of the tallest dog breeds, Great Danes are known as a 'gentle giant' but their size comes with a shorter lifespan and elevated bloat risk.

name: Shih Tzu
overview: Bred in Tibet and refined in China as a companion breed for royalty, the Shih Tzu's flat face and long coat both require regular attention.

name: Pomeranian
overview: A descendant of larger sled-pulling spitz breeds, the Pomeranian was bred down in size over centuries and remains one of the most popular toy breeds.

name: Rottweiler
overview: Descended from Roman cattle-driving dogs, Rottweilers are powerful working dogs that need structured training and socialization from an early age.

name: Yorkshire Terrier
overview: Originally bred in 19th-century England to catch rats in textile mills, Yorkies are now primarily companion dogs despite their terrier instincts.
